pages.title: About - Rachel Taylor MP
pages.meta_description: Rachel Taylor Labour MP for North Warwickshire and Bedworth
pages.text_excerpt: About - Rachel Taylor MP Skip to main content Rachel Taylor MP Rachel Taylor Labour MP for North Warwickshire and Bedworth Join Get Involved Donate About HS2 Get involved Monthly newsletter Contact News My views Surgeries and events More Menu About Rachel Taylor MP Rachel Taylor MP was elected as the Labour MP for North Warwickshire and Bedworth at the 2024 general election, overturning a majority of 17,956 votes to take the seat from the Conservatives. Prior to that, she was a local councillor in North Warwickshire and worked as a property solicitor with a high street firm in the constituency, while also previously serving as a line judge and umpire at Wimbledon and in grassroots tennis. She has also served as Vice President of the National Union of Students. Since joining Parliament, Rachel has become the Chair of the APPG for Freight and Logistics, become a member of the Women and Equality Select Committee, and championed LGBTQ+ rights and greater inclusion in sport. She has also launched campaigns to tackle freight crime, improve local bus services in North Warwickshire and Bedworth, and tackle disruption and poor public engagement by HS2. She also secured £20m from the government to rejuvenate Bedworth Town Centre. Rachel Taylor MP Contact details Email: rachel.taylor.mp@parliament.uk Further details Promoted by Martin Short at 69 Ratcliffe Rd, Atherstone, CV9 1LF ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: Rachel holds constituency events in Bedworth and Kingsbury on the government’s SEND reforms - Rachel Taylor MP
pages.h1: Rachel holds constituency events in Bedworth and Kingsbury on the government’s SEND reforms
pages.meta_description: Rachel Taylor Labour MP for North Warwickshire and Bedworth
pages.text_excerpt: Rachel holds constituency events in Bedworth and Kingsbury on the government’s SEND reforms - Rachel Taylor MP Skip to main content Rachel Taylor MP Rachel Taylor Labour MP for North Warwickshire and Bedworth Join Get Involved Donate About HS2 Get involved Monthly newsletter Contact News My views Surgeries and events More Menu 1st May, 2026 Rachel holds constituency events in Bedworth and Kingsbury on the government’s SEND reforms Rachel has held two public meetings at Kingsbury School and Nicholas Chamberlaine School to discuss the government’s proposed changes to the SEND system with parents and teaching staff. The government is investing billions to support children with special educational needs and/or disabilities including £200 million to train teachers, £3.7 billion to create over 60,000 more specialist places and £1.8 billion to create our new ‘Experts at Hand’ service to bring education and health professionals into schools. Speaking after the events, Rachel said: “I want to thank everyone who took part in these events, they were really useful to understand your views. Delivering change in the SEND system to ensure it delivers for families and SEND children is one of my biggest priorities as an MP, so I’m pleased at the positive response these reforms have had and will be sharing all your feedback with the government.” If you were not able to attend and would like to share your views then you still have time to do so. You can submit your thoughts and any concerns to the consultation by clicking here. Rachel Taylor MP Contact details Email: rachel.taylor.mp@parliament.uk Further details Promoted by Martin Short at 69 Ratcliffe Rd, Atherstone, CV9 1LF ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: Rachel’s campaign leads to hate crime law being strengthened - Rachel Taylor MP
pages.h1: Rachel’s campaign leads to hate crime law being strengthened
pages.meta_description: Rachel Taylor Labour MP for North Warwickshire and Bedworth
pages.text_excerpt: Rachel’s campaign leads to hate crime law being strengthened - Rachel Taylor MP Skip to main content Rachel Taylor MP Rachel Taylor Labour MP for North Warwickshire and Bedworth Join Get Involved Donate About HS2 Get involved Monthly newsletter Contact News My views Surgeries and events More Menu 15th April, 2026 Rachel’s campaign leads to hate crime law being strengthened Hate crime law is to be strengthened so perpetrators face tougher sentences after a successful campaign led by Rachel in parliament. Rachel said it was unacceptable that up until now those who committed acts of racial and religious hatred received harsher punishments than those who committed hate crimes against LGBT and disabled people, which is why the law needed to be changed. Last year, Rachel introduced an amendment to the Crime and Policing Bill that would make all hate crimes – and not just those motivated by race and religion – ‘aggravated offences’ worthy of tougher sentences. Today (15 April), the North Warwickshire and Bedworth MP expressed her delight that the change she campaigned for has now been passed by MPs and will become law. She also welcomed the government’s move to introduce a new crime of hate motivated by misogyny to hate crime law for the first time. Rachel’s campaign was backed by 104 cross-party MPs, who added their name to the amendment ahead of a debate on the Crime and Policing Bill. During that debate, Rachel said: “ We must have hate crime laws that show that whether it is your race or your religion, your sexual orientation, your gender identity, or indeed your disability, that Britain is a country that will not tolerate it. That all hatred is equal. And that all those who commit vile acts of hatred will face the same grave consequences.” Rachel said: “My constituents in North Warwickshire and Bedworth need to know that they are protected from vile acts of hate, whether that’s racist hate or hate motivated by the fact they are gay or disabled. “It’s shocking that between March 2023 – 2024, over 40,000 hate crimes against people in Britain because of their sexual orientation, transgender identity, or disability were recorded by the police. I am absolutely delighted that the government have listened to me and the cross-party MPs who supported this vital change, and that MPs last night voted for that change to become law.” Rachel Taylor MP Contact details Email: rachel.taylor.mp@parliament.uk Further details Promoted by Martin Short at 69 Ratcliffe Rd, Atherstone, CV9 1LF ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved.
